content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN.

Chef wants to append a **non-negative** integer XXX to the array AAA such that the bitwise OR of the entire array becomes =Y= Y=Y i.e. (A1 ∣ A2 ∣ … ∣ AN ∣ X)=Y(A_1 \ | \ A_2 \ | \  \ldots \ | \ A_N \ | \ X) = Y(A1​ ∣ A2​ ∣ … ∣ AN​ ∣ X)=Y. (Here, ∣|∣ denotes the bitwise OR operation)

Determine the **minimum** possible value of XXX. If no possible value of XXX exists, output −1-1−1.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and YYY — the size of the array AAA and final bitwise OR of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum possible value of XXX for which (A1 ∣ A2 ∣ … ∣ AN ∣ X)=Y(A_1 \ | \ A_2 \ | \  \ldots \ | \ A_N \ | \ X) = Y(A1​ ∣ A2​ ∣ … ∣ AN​ ∣ X)=Y holds.


If no such value exists, output −1-1−1.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and Y — the size of the array and the required final bitwise OR. The second line contains N space-separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the minimum non-negative integer X such that (A1 | A2 | … | AN | X) = Y. If no such X exists output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: OR of array = 3|5|6|2 = 7. Missing bit 3 (value 8) → X = 8.
Test case 2: OR = 1|2|1 = 3. Y has bit 3 set while OR does not, and OR already has bits 0‑1 set which Y does not, so impossible → -1.
Test case 3: OR = 0, Y = 1 → missing bit 0 → X = 1.
Test case 4: OR = 7, Y = 7 → already equal → X = 0.

First test: OR = 7, Y = 7 → X = 0.
Second test: OR = 1, Y = 5 (101b). Bit 2 is missing, so X = 4.

solution.approach: The key insight is to find the bitwise OR of all elements in the array and then find the minimum value that needs to be added to achieve the required bitwise OR. This can be done by using the bitwise OR operator and the bitwise NOT operator.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) per test case, O(T * N) total. No precomputation is done.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — the dominant structure is a constant amount of space to store the input and output.
